subject: [PATCH man-pages v1 4/6] UFFDIO_API.2const: Document UFFD_FEATURE_RWP{,_ASYNC} and 1 << _UFFDIO_SET_MODE

reviews.author: Alejandro Colomar
reviews.summary:
Reviewer noted that the documentation for UFFD_FEATURE_RWP_ASYNC incorrectly states it requires UFFD_FEATURE_RWP to be set in the same UFFDIO_API call, when in fact they can be set independently.

Reviewer Alejandro Colomar pointed out that the comment about UFFD_FEATURE_RWP_ASYNC requiring UFFD_FEATURE_RWP to be set in the same UFFDIO_API call is incomplete, as it does not mention what happens if this condition is not met.
reviews.raw_body:
Hi Kiryl,

On 2026-05-25T13:28:14+0100, Kiryl Shutsemau wrote:

---

Have a lovely day!
Alex

reviews.author: Alejandro Colomar
reviews.summary: Reviewer Alejandro Colomar initially expressed concerns about splitting the patch into multiple parts but later reconsidered and agreed that it makes sense to submit them separately.
reviews.raw_body:
Actually, I retire this.  I think it makes sense as a single patch.

Cheers,
Alex

2026-05-25.patch_summary: This patch updates the documentation for the UFFDIO_API(2const) man page to include two new feature bits: UFFD_FEATURE_RWP and UFFD_FEATURE_RWP_ASYNC, which were introduced in Linux 7.2. These features enable read-write protection tracking and in-kernel resolution of RWP faults without notification. The patch also documents the ability to toggle this asynchronous mode at runtime using the UFFDIO_SET_MODE(2const) ioctl.
